mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) directly drives corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with accelerating GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals tight labor markets that support wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and core inflation compress equity valuations by raising discount rates and eroding real margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently for three consecutive months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the signal reverses sign during high-volatility market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) validates economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth months produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over 50% of cases, or the predictive relationship vanishes when controlling for GDPC1 revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units signals robust consumer confidence and construction sector strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through wealth effects and downstream demand.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or zero in more than 55% of months where HOUST exceeds 1000, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent tightening of monetary policy (rising FEDFUNDS) and cooling labor markets (falling UNRATE) indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain stable when FEDFUNDS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously for two consecutive months, or the joint interaction term fails to significantly improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Declining residential mortgage debt outstanding (RSAFS) reflects credit contraction and reduced household leverage, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as consumption growth slows.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS contracts for three straight months, or the signal loses statistical significance after adjusting for CPIAUCSL trends.
